title: Finding
description: A single rule outcome emitted while generating a Table of Authorities. Findings are the canonical diagnostic unit; the Markdown report is a rendering of the generated TOA followed by zero or more findings. A finding is emitted only when there is something to disclose: a correction the tool made, a problem the user must act on, or a render caveat. It is never a legal judgment. Fields follow docs/PRD.md section 16 (no status or evaluation_path field).

finding_id.description: ULID identifying this finding instance. Sortable by creation time. Distinct from rule_id (which identifies the rule, not the finding).

rule_id.description: Identifier of the rule that produced this finding (e.g., TT-003). Matches the id field in the corresponding rule card.

rule_version.description: Semantic version of the rule card at generation time. Essential for reproducibility.

rule_pack_version.description: Semantic version of the toa rule pack at generation time.

profile_id.description: Identifier of the court profile in force during the run (e.g., 'frap'). Findings whose meaning depends on profile conventions (grouping, passim threshold) are only reproducible against the same profile version.

profile_version.description: Semantic version of the court profile in force during the run.

subject.description: Optional human-readable subject of the finding: the verbatim authority text, the input-TOA entry text, or the unparsed line the finding concerns. Present for per-authority and per-entry findings (TT-001, TT-002, TT-003, TT-004); omitted for run-level findings (TT-005, TT-006, TT-007, TT-008).

authority_id.description: Optional identifier of the registry Authority this finding concerns, when the finding maps to a resolved authority. Lets the report cross-reference findings to TOA entries. Omitted for unresolvable or run-level findings.

severity.description: What the finding demands of the user. 'error' = the tool could not safely produce part of its output, or produced output whose accuracy is in doubt, and the user must act (TT-001, TT-005, TT-007). 'warning' = the output may be affected and the user should verify before filing (TT-004, TT-006, TT-008). 'info' = a disclosure of normal behavior or a correction the tool made for you (TT-002 added a missing entry; TT-003 fixed stale page numbers).

confidence.description: Confidence in the finding's real-world claim. 'high' = determined from the document's structure and the converged render. 'medium' = depends on the citation parser's recall (deterministic over the parse, but only as good as eyecite; e.g. TT-004). 'low' = the rule applied but could not reach a stable conclusion. v1 rules use only 'high' and 'medium'.

message.description: Plain-language summary of the finding for the report. States what happened, what the tool did about it, and what (if anything) the user must do before filing.

evidence.description: Structured evidence supporting the finding, so a reviewer can audit it without leaving the report.

occurrence_references.description: Citation occurrences in the body that the finding cites as evidence. Empty when the finding concerns only the input TOA or run-level state.

paragraph_index.description: Index of the paragraph in the document body where the occurrence appears.

char_span.description: Inclusive-exclusive character offsets [start, end) of the occurrence within its paragraph.

page.description: Page on which the occurrence was located in the converged render. Null when the finding fired before a stable render was reached.

form.description: The citation form of this occurrence, as classified by eyecite resolution.

excerpt.description: Verbatim text of the occurrence. Never altered; truncation indicated by a trailing ellipsis.

toa_entry_references.description: Entries from the input document's existing TOA that the finding cites (e.g., a phantom or stale entry). Empty when the finding concerns only body occurrences or run-level state.

verbatim_text.description: The input TOA entry exactly as it appeared, including its page references. Never altered.

input_pages.description: Page references listed for this entry in the input TOA, when parseable.

parsed.description: Whether the entry could be interpreted as an authority plus page list. False for lines TT-004 reports as uninterpretable.

computed_values.description: Intermediate values the rule computed, exposed so a reviewer can audit its reasoning. Keys are rule-specific (e.g., 'corrected_pages', 'input_pages', 'occurrence_count', 'passim_threshold', 'iterations', 'substituted_fonts').

citations.description: Authorities supporting this finding, copied from the rule card. Required (non-empty) for error and warning findings; may be empty for info findings. Sources are rule/style authorities (FRAP, Bluebook) or the project's own normative specs - never case law about the user's matter.

url.description: Optional URL to the cited text.

description.description: Optional gloss on why this authority supports the finding.

remediation_hint.description: Optional plain-language guidance on what to check or do before filing. Clerical guidance only; not legal advice.

blocks_docx_output.description: Whether this finding caused the regenerated .docx to be suppressed (the report is still written). Denormalized from the rule card; true only for TT-005 (no placement found), where producing a .docx would mean guessing where the table goes.

evaluated_at_utc.description: When the rule was evaluated (not when the document was authored). Part of the run's audit trail.

engine_version.description: Semantic version of the toatool engine at generation time.
